name: Latency-Optimized Scheduling
description: Sub-sector template for low-latency and URLLC scheduling patents. Covers mini-slot scheduling, preemption, slot aggregation, and priority-based resource allocation for time-critical communication.
contextDescription: Latency-optimized scheduling is central to 5G NR URLLC and time-sensitive networking. Mini-slot scheduling (2, 4, or 7 symbols instead of 14) enables sub-millisecond latency. Preemption allows URLLC traffic to interrupt eMBB transmissions. These mechanisms are critical for industrial automation, autonomous vehicles, remote surgery, and XR/AR applications. Key targets: Qualcomm, Ericsson, Nokia, Samsung, all 5G infrastructure vendors. 3GPP TS 38.214 defines mini-slot and preemption procedures.
scoringGuidance: Mini-slot scheduling is mandatory for URLLC — every 5G NR gNB and URLLC UE implements it.
Preemption indication (DCI format 2_1) patents have broad infrastructure + device reach.
Slot aggregation and repetition patents improve reliability for URLLC.
Priority-based scheduling between eMBB and URLLC traffic is a key 5G feature.
Time-sensitive networking (TSN) integration with 5G scheduling is emerging high-value area.

questions.question: Does this patent address mini-slot (sub-slot) scheduling for reduced latency? Score 8-10 for novel mini-slot resource allocation or dynamic mini-slot/slot switching, 5-7 for mini-slot aware scheduling, 3-4 for general sub-frame scheduling, 1-2 for slot-level only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Mini-slot scheduling enables sub-1ms latency in 5G NR — identify symbol-level granularity.

questions.question: Does this patent address URLLC preemption of eMBB traffic (preemption indication, puncturing, resource reassignment)? Score 8-10 for novel preemption signaling or preemption-aware resource management, 5-7 for basic preemption handling, 3-4 for priority-based scheduling without preemption, 1-2 for no preemption aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Preemption is a novel 5G NR feature — eMBB traffic is interrupted for URLLC.

questions.question: Does this patent address ultra-reliability (repetition, slot aggregation, diversity) in scheduling? Score 8-10 for novel HARQ-less reliability techniques or multi-TRP diversity scheduling, 5-7 for repetition/aggregation optimization, 3-4 for general HARQ improvements, 1-2 for no reliability aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: URLLC requires 99.999% reliability — scheduling must guarantee delivery.

questions.question: How closely does this patent align with 3GPP URLLC/latency procedures (TS 38.214/38.321)? Score 8-10 for direct implementation of standardized URLLC behavior, 5-7 for enabling standard compliance, 3-4 for standard-adjacent, 1-2 for proprietary.
questions.reasoningPrompt: URLLC scheduling is standardized in 3GPP Rel-15/16/17 — SEP potential is high.

questions.question: Does this patent address integration with time-sensitive networking (TSN) or industrial automation scheduling? Score 8-10 for 5G-TSN bridge scheduling or deterministic communication, 5-7 for time-aware scheduling, 3-4 for general industrial awareness, 1-2 for no TSN/industrial aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: 5G-TSN integration is an emerging market for industrial automation.

questions.question: How detectable is this latency scheduling innovation? Score 8-10 for behavior observable in DCI (mini-slot grants, preemption indication), 5-7 for behavior measurable via latency testing, 3-4 for internal scheduler logic, 1-2 for algorithm-level only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Mini-slot grants and preemption indications are observable in DCI over the air.
